message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): expand article + ship downloadable guide; CSS for code blocks

Substantial content expansion of the Slack → Mattermost migration
post and addition of a downloadable companion guide.  The article
component grew by ~3.5k LoC of new content and the visualizations
component by ~2.4k LoC of new diagrams; the changes share one
intent so they're committed together.

components/slack-migration-article.tsx (+3555 LoC)
  Adds new sections covering: end-to-end workflow timeline; the
  rationale for self-hosting vs Slack pricing comparison (real
  April-2026 example); operational decisions per phase (export
  windowing, channel sidecars, Postgres bind options, rollback
  ownership); deeper coverage of the Phase 1 extraction pipeline
  (slackdump → mmetl → slack-advanced-exporter handoff,
  hash-stamped bundle assembly, machine-readable handoff.json);
  deeper coverage of the Phase 2 setup pipeline (Cloudflare DNS,
  Nginx WS proxy, PostgreSQL provisioning options, staging
  rehearsal, fail-closed readiness gate, smoke tests,
  reconciliation diffs, ROLLBACK_OWNER discipline).  Cross-links
  to the new visualizations and to the new downloadable guide.

components/slack-migration-visualizations.tsx (+2365 LoC)
  New illustrative diagrams: artifact-flow pipeline, idempotent
  JSONL replay structure, fail-closed gate decision tree,
  reconciliation diff dashboard sketch, cutover timeline, and
  delta-catchup-after-interruption flow.  Existing viz styles
  reorganized into the new `.sm-scope` namespacing introduced
  in the CSS below.

app/globals.css (+251 LoC, -0)
  New `.sm-scope` namespaced styles for the article: an
  `pre.sm-code-block` rule for multi-line command/config snippets
  with the JetBrains Mono fallback chain, the `(0,0,0,0.5)`
  backdrop, and `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` for iOS
  horizontal-scroll fluency.  One pre-existing rule flipped
  `overflow: hidden` → `overflow: clip` in the article wrapper to
  let internal `position: sticky` children survive their ancestor
  bounds (Safari-only nicety).

lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts
  Renamed the jargon-dictionary entry key from `fountain-code` to
  `fail-closed-gate` to match the term that's actually defined in
  the entry body (`Fail-Closed Gate`).  The original key was a
  drafting artefact from when the readiness gate borrowed the
  fountain-code metaphor.

public/slack-mattermost-migration-guide.md (NEW, 2551 lines)
  Standalone, downloadable companion guide intended to be a
  self-contained reference for admins running the migration without
  needing to keep the article open.  Covers: motivations and cost
  math; environment prerequisites; Phase 1 extraction (slackdump,
  mmetl, slack-advanced-exporter, hash-stamping, handoff.json
  schema); Phase 2 setup (server provisioning, Cloudflare proxy,
  Nginx config, PostgreSQL options, mmctl import, staging rehearsal,
  smoke tests, fail-closed readiness gate, ROLLBACK_OWNER pattern);
  cutover playbook; delta catch-up; rollback procedures; and
  appendices on Workflow Builder JSON migration and emoji
  reattachment.  Lives under public/ so it can be downloaded
  directly via the article's "Download the standalone guide" link
  without going through the Next.js app router.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
